perturbation.original_text: 3. Undertakings of the Parties The Parties undertake: i) to treat all Confidential Information as absolutely secret, private and confidential; ii) to take all steps necessary to preserve such confidentiality and secrecy; iii) to prevent disclosure of any such Confidential Information to any third party; iv) to use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose set out in Section 1; v) to disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its directors, employees and consultants who need the Confidential Information to conduct tasks in relation to the pur pose set out in Section 1; vi) to ascertain that each person who receives Confidential Information is made aware of and comply with this Agreement; and vii) to immediately upon request deliver to the other Party all Confidential Information that belong to that P arty in any form without retaining any copies thereof, or, at the other Party’s option, certify in writing that all copies of such information have been destroyed.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Undertakings of the Parties The Parties undertake: i) to treat all Confidential Information as absolutely secret, private and confidential, except as required by standard business practices; ii) to take all steps necessary to preserve such confidentiality and secrecy; iii) to prevent disclosure of any such Confidential Information to any third party; iv) to use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose set out in Section 1; v) to disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its directors, employees and consultants who need the Confidential Information to conduct tasks in relation to the pur pose set out in Section 1; vi) to ascertain that each person who receives Confidential Information is made aware of and comply with this Agreement; and vii) to immediately upon request deliver to the other Party all Confidential Information that belong to that P arty in any form without retaining any copies thereof, or, at the other Party’s option, certify in writing that all copies of such information have been destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: By adding 'except as required by standard business practices' to the undertaking to treat Confidential Information as secret, private, and confidential, a significant loophole is created. This phrase directly contradicts the initial undertaking. What constitutes 'standard business practices' is not defined, creating ambiguity and potential for dispute. This allows a party to argue that disclosing confidential information is permissible if they deem it a standard practice, directly undermining the confidentiality obligation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Undertakings of the Parties The Parties undertake: i) to treat all Confidential Information as absolutely secret, private and confidential, except as required by standard business practices; ii) to take all steps necessary to preserve such confidentiality and secrecy; iii) to prevent disclosure of any such Confidential Information to any third party; iv) to use the Confidential Information solely for the purpose set out in Section 1; v) to disclose the Confidential Information only to those of its directors, employees and consultants who need the Confidential Information to conduct tasks in relation to the pur pose set out in Section 1; vi) to ascertain that each person who receives Confidential Information is made aware of and comply with this Agreement; and vii) to immediately upon request deliver to the other Party all Confidential Information that belong to that P arty in any form without retaining any copies thereof, or, at the other Party’s option, certify in writing that all copies of such information have been destroyed.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'except as required by standard business practices' to section 3.i in the changed text directly contradicts the initial undertaking to treat all Confidential Information as absolutely secret, private and confidential. The term 'standard business practices' is undefined, creating ambiguity that allows a party to potentially disclose confidential information if they deem it a standard practice, undermining the confidentiality obligation. The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text in this case) highlights this contradiction because the initial absolute obligation is immediately qualified by an ambiguous exception, leading to a direct conflict within the same section.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Term and termination 8.1 This Agreement enters into force when signed by both Parties. The initial term of the Agreement is three (3) years. If the Agreement is not terminated by notice six (6) months prior to the end of each term, it shall be extended with twelve (12) months with the same notice period until terminated as aforesaid by any Party. However, all obligations of confidentiality and non -use will survive the termination of this Agreeme nt.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Term and termination 8.1 This Agreement enters into force when signed by both Parties. The initial term of the Agreement is three (3) years. If the Agreement is not terminated by notice six (6) months prior to the end of each term, it shall be extended with twelve (12) months with the same notice period until terminated as aforesaid by any Party. However, all obligations of confidentiality and non -use will not survive the termination of this Agreement for information that is publicly available.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'all obligations of confidentiality and non-use will survive the termination of this Agreement' to 'all obligations of confidentiality and non -use will not survive the termination of this Agreement for information that is publicly available,' creates an in-text contradiction in the duration of the confidentiality obligation. While the first part of the sentence implies that confidentiality obligations continue even after termination, the added clause suggests that confidentiality doesn't survive for public information, therefore creating contradiction and uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. Term and termination 8.1 This Agreement enters into force when signed by both Parties. The initial term of the Agreement is three (3) years. If the Agreement is not terminated by notice six (6) months prior to the end of each term, it shall be extended with twelve (12) months with the same notice period until terminated as aforesaid by any Party. However, all obligations of confidentiality and non -use will not survive the termination of this Agreement for information that is publicly available.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that "all obligations of confidentiality and non -use will survive the termination of this Agreement." The changed text modifies this to state that confidentiality and non-use obligations will "not survive the termination of this Agreement for information that is publicly available." This creates a contradiction because the original text implies perpetual confidentiality, while the changed text introduces an exception for publicly available information. The contradicted text is the changed text, so there is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Disputes and Gov erning Law 7.1 Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity hereof, shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of th e Stockholm Chamber of Commerce. The arbitral tribunal shall be composed of three arbitrators. The place of arbitration including the making of the award shall be Stockholm, Sweden. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Disputes and Gov erning Law 7.1 Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity hereof, shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of th e Stockholm Chamber of Commerce, except for disputes concerning intellectual property rights which shall be resolved in the courts of Sweden. The arbitral tribunal shall be composed of three arbitrators. The place of arbitration including the making of the award shall be Stockholm, Sweden. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that ALL disputes will be resolved through arbitration. By adding the exception 'except for disputes concerning intellectual property rights which shall be resolved in the courts of Sweden', a contradiction is created. This creates uncertainty about the dispute resolution mechanism, especially in cases where a dispute may involve both general contract issues and intellectual property aspects. This contradicts the original text
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Disputes and Gov erning Law 7.1 Any and all disputes, controversies and claims arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach, termination or invalidity hereof, shall be settled by arbitration in accordance with the Rules of the Arbitration Institute of th e Stockholm Chamber of Commerce, except for disputes concerning intellectual property rights which shall be resolved in the courts of Sweden. The arbitral tribunal shall be composed of three arbitrators. The place of arbitration including the making of the award shall be Stockholm, Sweden. The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that *any and all* disputes shall be settled by arbitration. The changed text introduces an exception for disputes concerning intellectual property rights, which will be resolved in the courts of Sweden. This creates a direct contradiction because the original text covers all disputes, while the changed text removes a specific type of dispute from that scope. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, highlighting that the change itself introduces the contradiction, making it a structural flaw. It alters the dispute resolution mechanism creating a direct conflict.

perturbation.original_text: 6. No license Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to co nstitute or to imply the granting of any license, immunity or other right under any intellectual property right of either Party, or to DOCUMENT NO. HDX4 -51-130 Replaces SCM -0021 LEGAL#1597040V1 provide a commitment of any kind by either Party to enter into further agreements with the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. No license Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to co nstitute or to imply the granting of any license, immunity or other right under any intellectual property right of either Party, or to DOCUMENT NO. HDX4 -51-130 Replaces SCM -0021 LEGAL#1597040V1 provide a commitment of any kind by either Party to enter into further agreements with the other Party. However, both parties agree to negotiate in good faith for licenses if required to achieve the purpose in Section 1.
perturbation.explanation: The initial clause explicitly states that the agreement does not imply any commitment to future agreements. By adding 'However, both parties agree to negotiate in good faith for licenses if required to achieve the purpose in Section 1', we introduce an element of obligation to negotiate future licenses. While it doesn't guarantee a license will be granted, the 'good faith' negotiation requirement contradicts the original statement that the agreement implies no such commitment, by putting legal pressure to comply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. No license Nothing in this Agreement shall be deemed to co nstitute or to imply the granting of any license, immunity or other right under any intellectual property right of either Party, or to DOCUMENT NO. HDX4 -51-130 Replaces SCM -0021 LEGAL#1597040V1 provide a commitment of any kind by either Party to enter into further agreements with the other Party. However, both parties agree to negotiate in good faith for licenses if required to achieve the purpose in Section 1.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 6 explicitly states that the agreement does not imply any commitment to future agreements. The changed text adds a clause that 'both parties agree to negotiate in good faith for licenses if required to achieve the purpose in Section 1'. This creates a direct contradiction because the new clause introduces an obligation to negotiate future licenses, which conflicts with the original statement that the agreement implies no such commitment. The contradicted text and the changed text are located at the same location, but the change introduces a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.
